untranslatable_reason: Signal requires ranking assets by 3-year cumulative return percentile and selecting extreme quintiles. This requires cross-asset ranking logic and portfolio-level calculations (percentile thresholds across asset universe) which cannot be expressed as single-instrument indicator conditions. The 200-day MA reversal confirmation is translatable, but the core mean-reversion rank-and-trade logic requires portfolio construction rules outside the scope of per-instrument technical conditions.
translation_notes: Attempted partial translation: Used returns indicator to approximate 3-year trend (noting that returns should be calculated over 3-year window in backtest engine). Long entry uses negative returns (bottom 20th percentile proxy) with price above 200-day MA confirmation. Short entry uses positive returns (top 80th percentile proxy) with price below 200-day MA. Exit conditions approximate reversal into 40th percentile of 3-year range using returns thresholds. Hold period set to 730 days (2 years). Core percentile ranking across asset universe requires explicit portfolio ranking logic not available in single-instrument rule syntax.
